blurb: Cohere is an enterprise-focused frontier AI company (LLMs, retrieval, agents) with a London presence, positioning around 'sovereign' and secure enterprise AI deployment.

publications.title: Introducing Command A+: Making sovereign agentic capabilities available to all
publications.summary: Launch of Command A+, framed around 'sovereign agentic capabilities' — relevant to the AI-sovereignty policy debate (national control over models/data) that Cohere positions itself within.

publications.title: Why Cultural Awareness is Essential for Global AI
publications.summary: Cohere argument on how users worldwide are affected by AI's cultural gaps — a values/representation angle adjacent to global AI governance. Listing showed no explicit date for this item; date left null.

controversies.title: Advance Local Media LLC et al. v. Cohere Inc. — news-publisher coalition copyright & trademark suit (S.D.N.Y., 1:25-cv-01305)
controversies.summary: A coalition of news and magazine publishers — including Advance Local, Advance Magazine Publishers (Condé Nast), The Atlantic, Forbes, Guardian News & Media, Los Angeles Times, Politico, Insider, Newsday, McClatchy, Vox and others — sued Cohere in the Southern District of New York on 13 Feb 2025, alleging copyright infringement (including via training-data copying and retrieval-augmented generation that produces verbatim or substitutive summaries of their articles) and trademark infringement / false designation of origin tied to hallucinated articles displayed alongside the publishers' marks. On 13 November 2025 Judge Colleen McMahon denied Cohere's motion to dismiss, holding the publishers had plausibly stated claims for direct copyright infringement (incl. 'substitutive summaries'), secondary infringement (inducement/contributory), and Lanham Act trademark violations. No merits ruling has been entered; the case is proceeding. Cohere has a London office and Guardian News & Media is a UK plaintiff, giving this a UK-adjacent dimension.
controversies.rawExcerpt: District court denies AI developer Cohere’s motion to dismiss, finding that news publishers plausibly stated claims for direct copyright infringement, including for “substitutive summaries” generated by Cohere’s AI models, for secondary copyright infringement based on inducement and contributory liability theories, and for trademark infringement and false designation of origin based on the display of plaintiffs’ trademarks along with hallucinated articles.

news.title: Why Cultural Awareness is Essential for Global AI
news.summary: Cohere on how users worldwide are affected by AI's cultural gaps — a values/representation angle adjacent to global AI governance and the sovereignty positioning Cohere leans into.

news.title: Introducing North Mini Code: Cohere's first model for developers
news.summary: Cohere ships North Mini Code, an agentic coding model — a product launch, included for newsroom completeness; Cohere's enterprise/sovereign-AI positioning is the policy-relevant throughline.

notes: Harvested 2026-06-27 via OpenClaw headless browser (cohere.com/blog passed cleanly; was untested by web_fetch this morning). Cohere's blog is mostly product/model launches (low policy signal) — I selected only the two items with a sovereignty/governance angle to keep signal high per SOUL ('skim everything, summarise what matters'). Dates taken from the date string shown inline on the blog listing (e.g. 'MAY 20, 2026'); the cultural-awareness post had no inline date so date=null. Omitted pure product launches (North Mini Code, Transcribe, Model Vault, Rerank 4, serving-fairness) as not policy-relevant.
